items.title_en: Subsistence economy: gathering: Zero to 5 percent dependence
items.title_ja: 生計経済：採集依存度0～5%
items.desc_en: Percentage dependence on gathering. Coded value: 0. Description: Zero to 5 percent dependence.
items.desc_ja: 採集活動への依存度が0～5%の生計経済形態。農業や狩猟など他の生計方法が主体。

items.title_en: Subsistence economy: gathering: Zero to 5 percent dependence
items.title_ja: 生計経済：採集依存度0～5%
items.desc_en: Percentage dependence on gathering. Coded value: 0. Description: Zero to 5 percent dependence.
items.desc_ja: 採集活動への依存度が0～5%の生計経済形態。農業や狩猟など他の生計方法が主体。

items.title_en: Weaving: Virilocal. Equivalent to "patrilocal" but confined to instances where the husband's patrikin are not aggregated in patrilocal and patrilineal kin groups
items.title_ja: 織物における夫居住制
items.desc_en: Type of weaving practiced. Coded value: 10. Description: Virilocal. Equivalent to "patrilocal" but confined to instances where the husband's patrikin are not aggregated in patrilocal and patrilineal kin groups.
items.desc_ja: 夫の父系血族が父系的に集約されていない場合に適用される、家父長居住制と等価な居住慣行。

items.title_en: Weaving: Patrilocal, i.e., normal residence with or near the male patrilineal kinsmen of the husband. Cf. V Virilocal
items.title_ja: 織物における家父長居住制
items.desc_en: Type of weaving practiced. Coded value: 8. Description: Patrilocal, i.e., normal residence with or near the male patrilineal kinsmen of the husband. Cf. V Virilocal.
items.desc_ja: 夫の父系血族と共に、または近くに居住する家父長的な住居慣行に関連する織物実践の分類。

items.title_en: Leather working: Wife to husband's group (patrilocal, virilocal) or wife to husband's mother's brother's household (avunculocal)
items.title_ja: 皮革加工：夫の集団または母方叔父の家族への妻の居住
items.desc_en: Leather working techniques. Coded value: 1. Description: Wife to husband's group (patrilocal, virilocal) or wife to husband's mother's brother's household (avunculocal).
items.desc_ja: 皮革加工技術と関連する居住パターン。妻が夫の集団（父系居住）または夫の母方叔父の家族（叔父系居住）に属する慣行。

items.title_en: Leather working: Wife to husband's group (patrilocal, virilocal) or wife to husband's mother's brother's household (avunculocal)
items.title_ja: 皮革加工：夫の集団または母方叔父の家族への妻の居住
items.desc_en: Leather working techniques. Coded value: 1. Description: Wife to husband's group (patrilocal, virilocal) or wife to husband's mother's brother's household (avunculocal).
items.desc_ja: 皮革加工技術と関連する居住パターン。妻が夫の集団（父系居住）または夫の母方叔父の家族（叔父系居住）に属する慣行。

items.title_en: Postpartum sex taboos: Trance explained as due to possession is known to occur, and there are no other trance states, but cases of possession outside of trance are also believed to occur.
items.title_ja: 産後のタブー：憑依による恍惚状態
items.desc_en: Cultural practices around childbirth. Coded value: 6. Description: Trance explained as due to possession is known to occur, and there are no other trance states, but cases of possession outside of trance are also believed to occur..
items.desc_ja: 出産後の文化的実践。憑依による恍惚状態が知られており、恍惚状態以外の憑依事例も信じられている。

items.title_en: Postpartum sex taboos: Two types of trance states are known to occur. One which is explained as due to possession and one which is given another type of explanation. In addition to explaining trance, possession belief also refers to one or more other phenomena.
items.title_ja: 産後のタブー：複数型の恍惚状態
items.desc_en: Cultural practices around childbirth. Coded value: 4. Description: Two types of trance states are known to occur. One which is explained as due to possession and one which is given another type of explanation. In addition to explaining trance, possession belief also refers to one or more other pheno
items.desc_ja: 出産後の文化的実践。憑依による恍惚状態と別の説明による恍惚状態の2種類が知られている。

items.title_en: Pottery: Virilocal. Equivalent to "patrilocal" but confined to instances where the husband's patrikin are not aggregated in patrilocal and patrilineal kin groups
items.title_ja: 陶芸：配偶者夫側居住（ビリロケール）
items.desc_en: Type of pottery practiced. Coded value: 10. Description: Virilocal. Equivalent to "patrilocal" but confined to instances where the husband's patrikin are not aggregated in patrilocal and patrilineal kin groups.
items.desc_ja: 夫の父系親族が聚居しない場合の配偶者夫側居住。父方居住に相当するが、父系集団が存在しない場合の形態。

items.title_en: Pottery: Patrilocal, i.e., normal residence with or near the male patrilineal kinsmen of the husband. Cf. V Virilocal
items.title_ja: 陶芸：父系出居婚
items.desc_en: Type of pottery practiced. Coded value: 8. Description: Patrilocal, i.e., normal residence with or near the male patrilineal kinsmen of the husband. Cf. V Virilocal.
items.desc_ja: 夫の父系親族の近くに住む居住パターンに関連した陶芸の実践形態。コード値8。

items.title_en: Boat building: Couple to either group or neolocal
items.title_ja: 舟建造：夫婦が集団またはネオローカル
items.desc_en: Types of watercraft constructed. Coded value: 2. Description: Couple to either group or neolocal.
items.desc_ja: 建造される水上交通具の類型。新婚夫婦の居住形態によるバリエーション。

items.title_en: Boat building: Couple to either group or neolocal
items.title_ja: 舟建造：夫婦が集団またはネオローカル
items.desc_en: Types of watercraft constructed. Coded value: 2. Description: Couple to either group or neolocal.
items.desc_ja: 建造される水上交通具の類型。新婚夫婦の居住形態によるバリエーション。

items.title_en: Settlement patterns: Exogamous communities, i.e., those revealing a marked tendency toward local exogamy without having the specific structure of clans
items.title_ja: 集落パターン：外婚制コミュニティ
items.desc_en: Type of settlement pattern. Coded value: 4. Description: Exogamous communities, i.e., those revealing a marked tendency toward local exogamy without having the specific structure of clans.
items.desc_ja: 氏族構造を持たない局所的外婚傾向を示すコミュニティの集落形態。

items.title_en: Settlement patterns: Agamous communities without localized clans or any marked tendency toward either local exogamy or local endogamy
items.title_ja: 集落パターン：無配偶コミュニティ
items.desc_en: Type of settlement pattern. Coded value: 3. Description: Agamous communities without localized clans or any marked tendency toward either local exogamy or local endogamy.
items.desc_ja: 局所化された氏族や外婚・内婚傾向を示さないコミュニティの集落形態。

items.title_en: Community size: None
items.title_ja: コミュニティ規模：なし
items.desc_en: Average community population. Coded value: 1. Description: None.
items.desc_ja: 血統集団による社会組織が存在しない、または記録されていないコミュニティ形態。

items.title_en: Community size: Sibs ("clans" in British usage), i.e., lineages whose core membership normally comprises residents of more than one community
items.title_ja: コミュニティ規模：シブ（氏族）
items.desc_en: Average community population. Coded value: 4. Description: Sibs ("clans" in British usage), i.e., lineages whose core membership normally comprises residents of more than one community.
items.desc_ja: 複数のコミュニティに居住する血統集団（氏族）を基本構成とする社会組織形態。

items.title_en: Subsistence economy: hunting: Zero to 5 percent dependence
items.title_ja: 生計経済：狩猟：0～5%依存度
items.desc_en: Percentage dependence on hunting. Coded value: 0. Description: Zero to 5 percent dependence.
items.desc_ja: 狩猟への依存度を示す指標。0～5%の低依存レベルを表すコード値0に分類される。

items.title_en: Subsistence economy: hunting: Zero to 5 percent dependence
items.title_ja: 生計経済：狩猟：0～5%依存度
items.desc_en: Percentage dependence on hunting. Coded value: 0. Description: Zero to 5 percent dependence.
items.desc_ja: 狩猟への依存度を示す指標。0～5%の低依存レベルを表すコード値0に分類される。

items.title_en: Agriculture type: Intensive cultivation where it is largely dependent upon irrigation
items.title_ja: 農業形態：灌漑依存型集約農業
items.desc_en: Type of agriculture practiced. Coded value: 6. Description: Intensive cultivation where it is largely dependent upon irrigation.
items.desc_ja: 灌漑に大きく依存した集約的な耕作方法。水管理技術を中心とした農業形態。

items.title_en: Agriculture type: Intensive agriculture on permanent fields, utilizing fertilization by compost or animal manure, crop rotation, or other techniques so that fallowing is either unnecessary or is confined to relatively short periods
items.title_ja: 農業形態：永年地集約農業
items.desc_en: Type of agriculture practiced. Coded value: 5. Description: Intensive agriculture on permanent fields, utilizing fertilization by compost or animal manure, crop rotation, or other techniques so that fallowing is either unnecessary or is confined to relatively short periods.
items.desc_ja: 堆肥・家畜肥料・輪作などの技術を用いて長期間同じ土地を耕作する集約農業形態。

items.title_en: Agriculture: intensity: Cereal grains, e.g., maize, millet, rice, or wheat, when at least as important as any other type of crop
items.title_ja: 農業: 集約度: 穀物主体
items.desc_en: Intensity of agricultural practice. Coded value: 6. Description: Cereal grains, e.g., maize, millet, rice, or wheat, when at least as important as any other type of crop.
items.desc_ja: 穀物（トウモロコシ、キビ、米、小麦など）が最も重要な作物の場合の農業集約度（コード値: 6）。

items.title_en: Agriculture: intensity: Roots or tubers, e.g., manioc, potatoes, taro, or yams, when more important than cereal grains and at least as important as tree crops or vegetables
items.title_ja: 農業: 集約度: 根菜・芋類主体
items.desc_en: Intensity of agricultural practice. Coded value: 5. Description: Roots or tubers, e.g., manioc, potatoes, taro, or yams, when more important than cereal grains and at least as important as tree crops or vegetables.
items.desc_ja: 根菜類（キャッサバ、ジャガイモ、タロ、ヤムイモなど）が穀物より重要な場合の農業集約度（コード値: 5）。

items.title_en: Subsistence economy: fishing: Zero to 5 percent dependence
items.title_ja: 生存経済：漁業依存度0～5%
items.desc_en: Percentage dependence on fishing. Coded value: 0. Description: Zero to 5 percent dependence.
items.desc_ja: 漁業への依存度を示す指標。コード値0は、生計の0～5%が漁業に依存していることを示す。

items.title_en: Subsistence economy: fishing: 16 to 25 percent dependence
items.title_ja: 生存経済：漁業依存度16～25%
items.desc_en: Percentage dependence on fishing. Coded value: 2. Description: 16 to 25 percent dependence.
items.desc_ja: 漁業への依存度を示す指標。コード値2は、生計の16～25%が漁業に依存していることを示す。

items.title_en: House construction: wall material: Four levels (e.g., large states)
items.title_ja: 住宅建設：壁材：四段階の政治組織
items.desc_en: Wall construction material and technique. Coded value: 5. Description: Four levels (e.g., large states).
items.desc_ja: 大規模国家など、四段階の政治的階層構造を持つ社会における壁材料と建設技術。

items.title_en: House construction: wall material: Two levels (e.g., larger chiefdoms)
items.title_ja: 住宅建設：壁材：二段階の政治組織
items.desc_en: Wall construction material and technique. Coded value: 3. Description: Two levels (e.g., larger chiefdoms).
items.desc_ja: 大規模酋長領など、二段階の政治的階層構造を持つ社会における壁材料と建設技術。

items.title_en: High god: A high god present but otiose or not concerned with human affairs
items.title_ja: 至高神：存在するが不活動的または人間関係に無関心
items.desc_en: Concept of a high god or supreme deity. Coded value: 2. Description: A high god present but otiose or not concerned with human affairs.
items.desc_ja: 至高神は存在するが、無関心またはオティオース（機能しない）で人間の事柄と関わらない。

items.title_en: High god: A high god absent or not reported in substantial descriptions of religious beliefs
items.title_ja: 至高神：不在または未報告
items.desc_en: Concept of a high god or supreme deity. Coded value: 1. Description: A high god absent or not reported in substantial descriptions of religious beliefs.
items.desc_ja: 宗教的信念の実質的な説明では至高神が不在または報告されていない状態。

items.title_en: House construction: shape: Games of all three types present
items.title_ja: 住宅建設：形状：三種類すべてのゲーム存在
items.desc_en: Architectural form of dwelling. Coded value: 8. Description: Games of all three types present.
items.desc_ja: 住宅の建築形態。戦略、身体スキル、偶然の三種類すべてのゲームが存在。

items.title_en: House construction: shape: Games of strategy only, involving no significant element of physical skill, e.g., chess, go, poker. Whether or not an element of chance is also involved is considered irrelevant
items.title_ja: 住宅建設：形状：戦略ゲームのみ
items.desc_en: Architectural form of dwelling. Coded value: 5. Description: Games of strategy only, involving no significant element of physical skill, e.g., chess, go, poker. Whether or not an element of chance is also involved is considered irrelevant.
items.desc_ja: 住宅の建築形態。戦略のみを用いるゲーム（チェス、囲碁、ポーカーなど）で、物理的スキルは不要。

items.title_en: Domestic animals: cattle: Animals employed in plow cultivation prior to the contact period
items.title_ja: 家畜：牛：接触前の耕作で使用された動物
items.desc_en: Presence and type of cattle. Coded value: 3. Description: Animals employed in plow cultivation prior to the contact period.
items.desc_ja: 接触前の時点で耕作に従事していた牛の存在を示す。農業技術と生産体系の指標。

items.title_en: Domestic animals: cattle: Animals employed in plow cultivation prior to the contact period
items.title_ja: 家畜：牛：接触前の耕作で使用された動物
items.desc_en: Presence and type of cattle. Coded value: 3. Description: Animals employed in plow cultivation prior to the contact period.
items.desc_ja: 接触前の時点で耕作に従事していた牛の存在を示す。農業技術と生産体系の指標。

items.title_en: Subsistence economy: animal husbandry: 6 to 15 percent dependence
items.title_ja: 生計経済：畜産依存度6～15%
items.desc_en: Percentage dependence on animal husbandry. Coded value: 1. Description: 6 to 15 percent dependence.
items.desc_ja: 畜産活動への依存度が6～15%の範囲にある生計経済形態。コード値1。

items.title_en: Subsistence economy: animal husbandry: 16 to 25 percent dependence
items.title_ja: 生計経済：畜産依存度16～25%
items.desc_en: Percentage dependence on animal husbandry. Coded value: 2. Description: 16 to 25 percent dependence.
items.desc_ja: 畜産活動への依存度が16～25%の範囲にある生計経済形態。コード値2。

items.title_en: Domestic animals: sheep: Bovine animals, e.g., cattle, mithun, water buffaloes, yaks
items.title_ja: 家畜：羊：牛科動物（牛、ミティン、水牛、ヤクなど）
items.desc_en: Presence and type of sheep. Coded value: 7. Description: Bovine animals, e.g., cattle, mithun, water buffaloes, yaks.
items.desc_ja: 羊の存在と種類を分類。コード値7：牛、ミティン、水牛、ヤクなどの牛科動物の飼育。

items.title_en: Domestic animals: sheep: Bovine animals, e.g., cattle, mithun, water buffaloes, yaks
items.title_ja: 家畜：羊：牛科動物（牛、ミティン、水牛、ヤクなど）
items.desc_en: Presence and type of sheep. Coded value: 7. Description: Bovine animals, e.g., cattle, mithun, water buffaloes, yaks.
items.desc_ja: 羊の存在と種類を分類。コード値7：牛、ミティン、水牛、ヤクなどの牛科動物の飼育。

items.title_en: Domestic animals: goats: Domestic animals milked more often than sporadically
items.title_ja: 家畜：ヤギ：定期的な搾乳
items.desc_en: Presence and type of goats. Coded value: 2. Description: Domestic animals milked more often than sporadically.
items.desc_ja: ヤギの飼育と搾乳慣行。定期的に搾乳される家畜の存在を示す。

items.title_en: Domestic animals: goats: Absence or near absence of milking
items.title_ja: 家畜：ヤギ：搾乳の欠如または最小限
items.desc_en: Presence and type of goats. Coded value: 1. Description: Absence or near absence of milking.
items.desc_ja: ヤギの飼育。搾乳がほぼない、または全く行われていない地域を示す。

items.title_en: Domestic animals: pigs: Intensive agriculture contributes most
items.title_ja: 家畜：豚：集約農業が主要因
items.desc_en: Presence and type of pigs. Coded value: 7. Description: Intensive agriculture contributes most.
items.desc_ja: 豚の存在と種類。集約農業が最も寄与する農業形態を示す。

items.title_en: Domestic animals: pigs: Intensive agriculture contributes most
items.title_ja: 家畜：豚：集約農業が主要因
items.desc_en: Presence and type of pigs. Coded value: 7. Description: Intensive agriculture contributes most.
items.desc_ja: 豚の存在と種類。集約農業が最も寄与する農業形態を示す。

items.title_en: Subsistence economy: agriculture: 86 to 100 percent dependence
items.title_ja: 生計経済：農業依存度86～100%
items.desc_en: Percentage dependence on agriculture. Coded value: 9. Description: 86 to 100 percent dependence.
items.desc_ja: 農業への依存度を示す指標。依存度が86～100%の範囲を示すコード値9に分類される。

items.title_en: Subsistence economy: agriculture: 56 to 65 percent dependence
items.title_ja: 生計経済：農業依存度56～65%
items.desc_en: Percentage dependence on agriculture. Coded value: 6. Description: 56 to 65 percent dependence.
items.desc_ja: 農業への依存度を示す指標。依存度が56～65%の範囲を示すコード値6に分類される。

items.title_en: Kinship: descent type: Complex stratification into social classes correlated in large measure with extensive differentiation of occupational statuses
items.title_ja: 親族：系統：複雑な階級分化と職業分化
items.desc_en: System of descent reckoning. Coded value: 5. Description: Complex stratification into social classes correlated in large measure with extensive differentiation of occupational statuses.
items.desc_ja: 職業的地位の多様化と相関する複雑な社会階級への階層化システム。

items.title_en: Kinship: descent type: Dual stratification into a hereditary aristocracy and a lower class of ordinary commoners or freemen, where traditionally ascribed noble status is at least as decisive as control over scarce resources
items.title_ja: 親族：系統：二元的階層化（貴族と平民）
items.desc_en: System of descent reckoning. Coded value: 4. Description: Dual stratification into a hereditary aristocracy and a lower class of ordinary commoners or freemen, where traditionally ascribed noble status is at least as decisive as control over scarce resources.
items.desc_ja: 世襲的な貴族身分と平民階級への二元的な社会分化。身分が資源支配より重視される。

items.title_en: Kinship: cousin marriage: No secondary type
items.title_ja: 親族：いとこ婚：二次タイプなし
items.desc_en: Preferred cousin marriage type. Coded value: 9. Description: No secondary type.
items.desc_ja: 好まれるいとこ婚のタイプ。二次的な分類がない場合のコード値9。

items.title_en: Kinship: cousin marriage: No secondary type
items.title_ja: 親族：いとこ婚：二次タイプなし
items.desc_en: Preferred cousin marriage type. Coded value: 9. Description: No secondary type.
items.desc_ja: 好まれるいとこ婚のタイプ。二次的な分類がない場合のコード値9。

items.title_en: Kinship: community organization: Absence or near absence of slavery
items.title_ja: 親族：コミュニティ組織：奴隷制度の欠落または希少
items.desc_en: Community-level kinship organization. Coded value: 1. Description: Absence or near absence of slavery.
items.desc_ja: 奴隷制度がほぼ存在しない、またはまったく存在しないコミュニティの親族組織を分類するもの。

items.title_en: Kinship: community organization: Absence or near absence of slavery
items.title_ja: 親族：コミュニティ組織：奴隷制度の欠落または希少
items.desc_en: Community-level kinship organization. Coded value: 1. Description: Absence or near absence of slavery.
items.desc_ja: 奴隷制度がほぼ存在しない、またはまったく存在しないコミュニティの親族組織を分類するもの。

items.title_en: Political integration: jurisdictional hierarchy: Nonhereditary succession through election or some other mode of formal consensus
items.title_ja: 政治統合：管轄権階層：選挙による非世襲的継承
items.desc_en: Levels of political authority. Coded value: 6. Description: Nonhereditary succession through election or some other mode of formal consensus.
items.desc_ja: 政治的権威の段階を分類したもの。形式的合意または選挙による非世襲的後継制度。

items.title_en: Political integration: jurisdictional hierarchy: Nonhereditary succession through election or some other mode of formal consensus
items.title_ja: 政治統合：管轄権階層：選挙による非世襲的継承
items.desc_en: Levels of political authority. Coded value: 6. Description: Nonhereditary succession through election or some other mode of formal consensus.
items.desc_ja: 政治的権威の段階を分類したもの。形式的合意または選挙による非世襲的後継制度。

items.title_en: Metal working: Monogamous
items.title_ja: 金属加工：一夫一妻制
items.desc_en: Type of metal working practiced. Coded value: 1. Description: Monogamous.
items.desc_ja: 金属加工の実践形態。一夫一妻制の婚姻体制を特徴とする伝統的金属加工技術。

items.title_en: Metal working: Monogamous
items.title_ja: 金属加工：一夫一妻制
items.desc_en: Type of metal working practiced. Coded value: 1. Description: Monogamous.
items.desc_ja: 金属加工の実践形態。一夫一妻制の婚姻体制を特徴とする伝統的金属加工技術。

items.title_en: Furyu-odori, ritual dances imbued with people’s hopes and prayers
items.title_ja: 風流踊り：人々の願いと祈りが込められた儀式的踊り
items.desc_en: Furyu-odori in Japan refers to various ritual folk dances, characterized by eye-catching costumes and lively dances and music. These local dances have been performed for centuries, primarily in rural areas. With life in these areas often affected by severe climate events, local communities created r
items.desc_ja: 日本の農村地域で数世紀にわたり行われてきた儀式的民俗舞踊。華やかな衣装と軽快な踊りと音楽で特徴付けられ、厳しい気候条件に対抗して穏やかな生活を祈るために創造された。

items.title_en: Traditional knowledge and skills of sake-making with koji mold in Japan
items.title_ja: 日本の麹を用いた酒造りの伝統知識と技術
items.desc_en: Sake is an alcoholic beverage made from grains and water that is deeply rooted in Japanese culture. Craftspeople use koji mould to convert the starch in the ingredients into sugar. They oversee the process to make sure the mould grows in optimal conditions, adjusting the temperature and humidity as
items.desc_ja: 麹菌を用いてデンプンを糖に変える日本の酒造り。温度と湿度の調整により品質が決まり、祭りや人生儀礼で神聖な役割を果たす。

items.title_en: Traditional skills, techniques and knowledge for the conservation and transmission of wooden architecture in Japan
items.title_ja: 日本の木造建築の保存と伝承の伝統技術と知識
items.desc_en: The conservation and transmission of wooden architecture consists in a set of traditional skills, techniques and knowledge to build new wooden structures and restore existing ones. This includes skills such as plastering, the cultivation and harvesting of raw materials, lacquer painting of tradition
items.desc_ja: 木造建築の建設と修復に必要な伝統的な技術・知識。左官や漆塗装など多様な技能を含み、近代化で伝承が課題に。

items.title_en: Akiu no Taue Odori
items.title_ja: 秋居の田植え踊り
items.desc_en: In the Akiu no Taue Odori, residents of the town of Akiu in northern Japan pray for a good harvest by simulating in dance the actions involved in transplanting rice. Performed since the end of the seventeenth century in communities throughout the region, the Akiu no Taue Odori today takes place duri
items.desc_ja: 日本北部の秋居地方で行われる、稲作の動きを踊りで表現する豊作祈願の儀式。17世紀後期から実践されている。

items.title_en: Dainichido Bugaku
items.title_ja: 大日堂舞楽
items.desc_en: According to legend, travelling performers of ''bugaku,'' the ritual dance and music of the imperial palace, visited Hachimantai Town in northern Japan in the early eighth century, during the reconstruction of Dainichido, the shrine pavilion. The ritual performance of Dainichido Bugaku takes its nam
items.desc_ja: 8世紀に日本北部八幡平町で伝承され始めた宮廷儀式舞踊・音楽。4つの地域コミュニティで世代を超えて継承された地方的特徴を反映する伝統芸能。

items.title_en: Oku-noto no Aenokoto
items.title_ja: 奥能登の秋祭礼
items.desc_en: Oku-noto no Aenokoto is an agricultural ritual transmitted from generation to generation by the rice farmers of the Noto Peninsula, which projects from Ishikawa prefecture in the centre of Japan’s main island, Honshu. The twice-yearly ceremony is unique among the harvest rituals of Asia in that the
items.desc_ja: 石川県能登半島の稲作農民が世代間で伝承する農業儀礼。年2回の儀式で、家の主が田の神を家に招き、目に見えない精霊を現在のように扱う。

items.title_en: Traditional Ainu dance
items.title_ja: アイヌの伝統舞踊
items.desc_en: The Ainu are an indigenous people who today live mostly in Hokkaidō in northern Japan. Traditional Ainu dance is performed at ceremonies and banquets, as part of newly organized cultural festivals and privately in daily life; in its various forms, it is closely connected to the lifestyle and religio
items.desc_ja: 北海道に住むアイヌ民族の伝統舞踊。儀式や宴会での演技で、アイヌの生活様式と宗教に密接に関連。多くの踊りが動物の動きや声を模倣する。

items.title_en: Ningyo Johruri Bunraku puppet theatre
items.title_ja: 人形浄瑠璃文楽
items.desc_en: Ranking with Nô and Kabuki as one of Japan’s foremost stage arts, the Ningyo Johruri Bunraku puppet theatre is a blend of sung narrative, instrumental accompaniment and puppet drama. This theatrical form emerged during the early Edo period (ca. 1600) when puppetry was coupled with Johruri, a popular
items.desc_ja: 江戸初期（1600年頃）に成立した日本の代表的舞台芸術。語り部、楽器伴奏、人形操作が融合した歌唱劇で、歴史劇と現代劇を題材とする。

items.title_en: Yuki-tsumugi, silk fabric production technique
items.title_ja: 結城紬絹織物製造技術
items.desc_en: Yuki-tsumugi is a Japanese silk-weaving technique found principally in Yuki City and Oyama City, along the Kinu River, north of Tokyo. The region boasts a warm climate and fertile lands, which are ideal for the growth of mulberry trees and sericulture. The Yuki-tsumugi technique is employed to produ
items.desc_ja: 東京北部の結城市と小山市で実践される日本の絹織物技術。温暖な気候と肥沃な土地で桑栽培に適し、軽く温かみのある生糸を生産し着物に用いられる。

items.title_en: Washi, craftsmanship of traditional Japanese hand-made paper
items.title_ja: 和紙：日本伝統手漉き製紙技術
items.desc_en: Washi craftsmanship refers to the traditional practice of making paper by hand using the fibres of the paper mulberry plant. The fibres are first separated, then soaked in clear river water to thicken before being filtered through a bamboo screen. This traditional, hand-made paper is used not only f
items.desc_ja: 楮の繊維を水に浸して竹簀で漉くことで手作りされる伝統製紙技術。手紙や書籍だけでなく、障子や襖などの室内装飾に使用される。地域社会の多くの住民がこの伝承技術に携わっている。

items.title_en: Nachi no Dengaku, a religious performing art held at the Nachi fire festival
items.title_ja: 那智火祭の宗教芸能：那智の伝楽
items.desc_en: Nachi no Dengaku is a Japanese folk performing art with a deep connection to Kumano Sanzan, a sacred site in Nachisanku. It is performed on a stage inside Kumano Nachi Shrine during the annual Nachi Fire Festival, celebrated on 14 July. It is a key component of the festival and takes the form of rit
items.desc_ja: 和歌山の熊野那智大社で7月14日に行われる日本の民俗芸能。笛と太鼓のリズムで行われることぶき踊りで、豊作祈願の儀式的舞踊である。聖地熊野三山と深い関わりがある。

items.title_en: Sada Shin Noh, sacred dancing at Sada shrine, Shimane
items.title_ja: 貞奴神能（さだのかみのう）
items.desc_en: Sada Shin Noh comprises a series of ritual purification dances performed every year on 24 and 25 September at the Sada Shrine in Matsue City, Shimane Prefecture, Japan as part of the ''gozakae'' ritual of the changing of the rush mats. The dances are undertaken to purify new rush mats ''(goza)'', up
items.desc_ja: 島根県松江市の貞奴神社で毎年9月24日・25日に行われる神聖な儀式舞踊。新しい樹皮製の敷物を清めるための儀式の一部。

items.title_en: Daimokutate
items.title_ja: 大幕立
items.desc_en: In Yahashira Shrine of Nara City in central Japan, young men of the Kami-fukawa community stand in a semi-circle dressed in samurai clothes and carrying bows. One by one, they are called to the centre by an old man who reads the name of a character in the tales of the feud between the Genji and Heik
items.desc_ja: 奈良県の八柱神社で行われる伝統的な口承芸能。若い男性たちが源平合戦の登場人物になりきり、独特の方言で台詞を暗誦する儀式的パフォーマンス。

items.title_en: Gagaku
items.title_ja: 雅楽
items.desc_en: Gagaku, characterized by long, slow songs and dance-like movements, is the oldest of the Japanese traditional performing arts. It is performed at banquets and ceremonies in the Imperial Palace and in theatres throughout the country, and encompasses three distinct arts. The first, Kuniburi no Utamai,
items.desc_ja: 日本最古の伝統芸能。長く緩やかな歌と舞踏的な動きが特徴で、皇宮での宴や儀式で演じられる。古代日本の歌、器楽、儀式舞を統合した芸能。

items.title_en: Hayachine Kagura
items.title_ja: 早池峰神楽
items.desc_en: In the fourteenth or fifteenth century, when the people of Iwate Prefecture in the northern part of mainland Japan worshipped Mt. Hayachine as a deity, they began a tradition of folk performance that continues to enliven the Great Festival of the Hayachine Shrine held in Hanamaki City on the first d
items.desc_ja: 岩手県の早池峰山への信仰に由来する14～15世紀から続く民俗芸能。仮面をかぶった舞踏と太鼓、シンバル、笛による8月の祭りの儀式的パフォーマンス。

items.title_en: Ojiya-chijimi, Echigo-jofu: techniques of making ramie fabric in Uonuma region, Niigata Prefecture
items.title_ja: 小千谷縮・越後上布：新潟県魚沼地域の苧麻織物製造技術
items.desc_en: The high-quality, lightweight patterned textiles made from the ramie plant are ideal for the hot and humid Japanese summer. Ojiya-chijimi, Echigo-jofu: techniques of making ramie fabric in Uonuma region, Niigata Prefecture developed in the north-western part of Japan’s main island and bear the mark
items.desc_ja: 苧麻植物から作られた軽量で高品質な夏布。指の爪で繊維を裂き、手紡ぎ、絞り染めにより、新潟県の冬の気候特性を反映した伝統技法。

items.title_en: Nôgaku theatre
items.title_ja: 能楽
items.desc_en: Nôgaku theatre had its heyday in the fourteenth and fifteenth centuries, but actually originated in the eighth century when the Sangaku was transmitted from China to Japan. At the time, the term Sangaku referred to various types of performance featuring acrobats, song and dance as well as comic sket
items.desc_ja: 8世紀の中国から伝来した散楽に由来し、14～15世紀に全盛期を迎えた能楽は、日本古典劇の主要形式として他の伝統芸能に影響を与えている。

items.title_en: Kabuki theatre
items.title_ja: 歌舞伎
items.desc_en: Kabuki is a Japanese traditional theatre form, which originated in the Edo period at the beginning of the seventeenth century and was particularly popular among townspeople. Originally, both men and women acted in Kabuki plays, but eventually only male actors performed the plays: a tradition that ha
items.desc_ja: 江戸時代初期に発生した日本の伝統劇形式で、町人に人気があった。男性俳優のみが演じ、女性役を専門とする歌舞伎役者を女形と呼ぶ。

items.title_en: Kumiodori, traditional Okinawan musical theatre
items.title_ja: クミオドリ：沖縄の伝統音楽劇
items.desc_en: Kumiodori is a Japanese performing art found on the Okinawa islands. It is based upon traditional Okinawan music and dance, but also incorporates elements from mainland Japan, such as Nogaku or Kabuki, as well as from China. Kumiodori dramas recount local historical events or legends, accompanied by
items.desc_ja: 沖縄発祥の伝統音楽劇。沖縄音楽と舞踊を基盤としながら、能やカバキ、中国の要素も取り入れている。三弦楽器で伴奏し、琉球音階に基づいたリズムで古い言葉により地元の歴史や伝説を語る。

items.title_en: Yama, Hoko, Yatai, float festivals in Japan
items.title_ja: 日本の山鉾祭の浮き山車祭
items.desc_en: Yama, Hoko and Yatai float festivals are held annually in various regions to pray to the gods for peace and protection from natural disasters. A traditional practice that showcases the diversity of local cultures, the festivals involve the collaborative efforts of the practising communities and are
items.desc_ja: 日本各地で年1回開催される浮き山車祭。神への祈りと自然災害からの保護を目的とし、全年代が参加し設計から運営まで協働する。

items.title_en: Washoku, traditional dietary cultures of the Japanese, notably for the celebration of New Year
items.title_ja: 和食：日本の伝統的食文化
items.desc_en: Washoku is a social practice based on a set of skills, knowledge, practice and traditions related to the production, processing, preparation and consumption of food. It is associated with an essential spirit of respect for nature that is closely related to the sustainable use of natural resources. T
items.desc_ja: 食の生産から消費までを包括する日本の社会実践。自然への敬意に基づき、正月祝いを通じて自然資源の持続的利用と文化的特性が表れる。

items.title_en: Raiho-shin, ritual visits of deities in masks and costumes
items.title_ja: ライホシン：仮面と衣装の神霊訪問儀式
items.desc_en: Raiho-shin rituals take place annually in various regions of Japan – especially in the Tohoku, Hokuriku, Kyushu and Okinawa regions – on days that mark the beginning of the year or when the seasons change. Such rituals stem from folk beliefs that deities from the outer world – the Raiho-shin – visit
items.desc_ja: 日本の各地域で年初めや季節の変わり目に行われる儀式。外界の神ライホシンが新年や新季節の幸運をもたらすとの民間信仰に基づく。地元民が神の衣装と面をつけて家々を訪問し、戒めと祝福を与える。

items.title_en: Mibu no Hana Taue, ritual of transplanting rice in Mibu, Hiroshima
items.title_ja: 広島三布の花田植え儀式
items.desc_en: Mibu no Hana Taue is a Japanese agricultural ritual carried out by the Mibu and Kawahigashi communities in Kitahiroshima Town, Hiroshima Prefecture to assure an abundant rice harvest by celebrating the rice deity. On the first Sunday of June, after the actual rice transplanting has ended, the ritual
items.desc_ja: 広島県三布地域で6月初日曜に行われる水稲豊作祈願の農業儀式。実際の田植えの後、儀式的に各段階を再現し、牛を飾り立てて神社へ導く。
